task_completion.description: Open Juris-M and initiate a new Case entry
task_completion.visual_evidence: Juris-M application is open and a new item creation pane/window is visible, specifically set to the item type 'Case'.

task_completion.description: Enter the case title 'Roe v. Wade'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Roe v. Wade' is visible in the 'Case Name' or 'Title' field in the Juris-M right-hand info pane.

task_completion.description: Enter the Court and Date details
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'United States Supreme Court' is visible in the 'Court' field, and '1973' is visible in the 'Date' field.

task_completion.description: Enter the Reporter, Volume, and Pages details
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'U.S.' is visible in the 'Reporter' field, '410' in the 'Volume' field, and '113' in the 'Pages' field.

task_completion.description: Verify the case is added to the Juris-M library
task_completion.visual_evidence: The case 'Roe v. Wade' appears as an entry in the central library list view of the Juris-M application.

integrity.description: Agent used the Juris-M GUI to add the case
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Juris-M graphical interface, not terminal commands like sqlite3 or python scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Juris-M application
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being used is clearly Juris-M (a Zotero fork), identifiable by its layout, icons, and menus, rather than a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI to manipulate the database directly
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing direct SQLite database inserts or modifications to the zotero.sqlite file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected values to verify in the Juris-M info pane: Title/Case Name: 'Roe v. Wade', Court: 'United States Supreme Court', Date: '1973', Reporter: 'U.S.', Volume: '410', Pages: '113'.
